summary: Prove persisted thumbnail bytes before project reopen while allowing valid post-open PNG re-encoding.

decisions.decision: In the fresh packaged-WebView process, inspect and hash the unique persisted thumbnail record before reopening the project and require that digest to equal the pre-restart PNG digest. After reopening and rendering, require the same canonical geometry identity plus valid bounded PNG dimensions rather than identical compressed PNG bytes. Close Welcome before post-reopen assertions so failure cleanup can always restore the source.
decisions.rationale: Reopening intentionally starts a new render and WebGL PNG encoding is not byte-deterministic across processes. The pre-reopen storage digest proves the original bytes survived restart; canonical geometry identity proves the replacement image represents the same accepted geometry. Requiring re-encoded bytes to match exactly was a false persistence oracle and could also strand cleanup behind the Welcome overlay.

testEvidence.criterion: Restart evidence must include and revalidate the pre-reopen persisted PNG digest, while the post-reopen comparison must use canonical geometry identity.
testEvidence.test: Extend the walkthrough, retained verifier, and packaged-runner contract tests with persistedThumbnailSha256 and an ordering assertion that storage inspection precedes project reopen.

red.outcome: RED: 3 failed, 18 passed because the existing restart evidence omitted the required persisted-thumbnail digest.

green.outcome: GREEN: 76/76 tests passed, including the pre-reopen ordering contract and rejection of a replacement persisted-thumbnail digest.

nearMisses.sought: Exact clean packaged native M4 restart and thumbnail-persistence evidence
nearMisses.confirmation: The run passed build, cube/STL, MCP, cache, geometry delta, animation, and thumbnail validation, then failed because a fresh-process re-render produced a different valid PNG byte stream for the same canonical geometry; cleanup passed.

questions: No owner decision is required; the change strengthens the persistence proof while removing an unsupported PNG-encoding determinism assumption.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
